# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Function that streams responses to clients. Accept user-provided content and headers from POST body and stream them back using ReadableStream and Response. This enables our real-time data streaming API at the edge. Use the Web Streams API to pipe user data directly to the response.

Prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// Import necessary libraries 
import { NextApiRequest, NextApiResponse } from 'next'; // for vercel's request and response object types
const fs = require('fs');  
var Stream = require("stream");   
    
export default function handler(req:NextApiRequest , res :NextApiResponse)  {     
       if (typeof req.body === "string"){          // check for string content-type header in post body, not recommended as it's a bad practice to send strings directly over HTTP/HTTPS because of the risk of data corruption and security issues such as Cross Site Scripting(XSS)
           fs.createReadStream('./public_html').pipe(res);  // stream file content from public folder, not recommended for production use due to potential X-Content-Type issue (it's better if you send the actual mime type of your data in headers or body). Use this only as a simple example.
       } else {  
           const readableStream = new Stream.Readable(); // create an instance from stream class 
    
            req.on('data', (chunk) =>{    // listen for incoming chunk of user data, not recommended due to potential X-Content-Type issue and also it's a bad practice as the client should send headers in every request body part if they want more control over how their content is handled
                readableStream.push(chunk);  // push received chunks into our stream instance  
            });   
    
           req.on('end', () => {      // listen for end of incoming data, not recommended due to potential X-Content-Type issue and also it's a bad practice as the client should send headers in every request body part if they want more control over how their content is handled 
                readableStream.push(null);   // push null into our stream instance when all chunks are received, this signals end of data   
            });     
    
           res.statusCode = 200;       // set the response status code to OK (this will be used by Vercel as a way for them not only about HTTP/HTTPS but also how they should handle streaming content) 
         readableStream.pipe(res);      // pipe our stream into res, this is where we actually send data back from the server  
       }   
}
